title: St. Mary's Glacier Hike
description: A short but rewarding climb to a year-round snowfield and alpine lake near Idaho Springs. Bring layers — it's brisk at 10,400 ft even in July.

title: Sunrise at Rocky Mountain National Park
description: Beat the crowds and the afternoon storms. Catch first light over Bear Lake, then wander the Glacier Gorge trails as the peaks turn gold.

title: Mount Falcon Sunset Hike
description: Foothills trail with sweeping views of Red Rocks and the Denver skyline. Time it for golden hour and pack a thermos.

title: Chautauqua Park Sunrise Picnic
description: Spread a blanket beneath the Flatirons in Boulder. Easy meadow trails if you want to stretch your legs after coffee and pastries.

title: Ice Skating on Evergreen Lake
description: The largest groomed outdoor ice rink in the country. Rent skates, do a few laps, then warm up with cocoa at the lake house.

title: Royal Arch Trail, Boulder
description: A leg-burning climb to a natural sandstone arch framing the plains. Steep but short — the payoff view is worth every switchback.

title: Hanging Lake Trail (Glenwood)
description: A turquoise travertine lake fed by waterfalls, reached by a steep mile-long climb. Permit required — plan ahead for this bucket-list gem.

title: Pumpkin Patch at Anderson Farms
description: Corn maze, hayrides, and a sea of pumpkins in Erie. Peak fall energy with cider donuts to match.

title: Cherry Creek Arts Festival
description: Denver's premier outdoor arts festival over July 4th weekend — 250+ artists, live music, and food trucks in Cherry Creek North.

title: Chatfield Sunflower Festival
description: Acres of sunflowers in golden bloom at Chatfield Farms. Bring a camera — the late-summer light through the petals is unreal.

title: Breckenridge Snow Sculpture Championships
description: Watch international teams carve 20-ton blocks of snow into towering art. A magical, free winter spectacle in a ski town.

title: Maroon Bells Fall Foliage Drive
description: The most photographed peaks in North America, mirrored in Maroon Lake and framed by golden aspens. A road-trip worth every mile.

title: Blossoms of Light, Botanic Gardens
description: A million holiday lights wind through Denver Botanic Gardens after dark. Cozy, dazzling, and made for slow strolls and hand-holding.

title: Great Divide Brewing Tour
description: Tour the RiNo barrel room and sip award-winning Yeti stouts. An easy, sociable date a short walk from Denver Union Station.

title: Odell Brewing Taproom, Fort Collins
description: Sprawling patio, rotating pilot batches, and food trucks in beer-loving Fort Collins. Make a day of it with the FoCo brewery district.

title: Denver Food Hall Crawl
description: Graze your way through Zeppelin Station and Denver Central Market — small plates, natural wine, and oysters, all walkable in RiNo.

title: Farm-to-Table Dinner at Mercantile
description: Alex Seidel's elegant market-restaurant inside Union Station. A splurge-worthy tasting of Colorado ingredients at their peak.

title: Fireside Hot Chocolate, Dillon Reservoir
description: Wrap up a mountain day with cocoa by the water in Dillon or Frisco. Snowcapped peaks, string lights, and zero agenda.

title: Larimer Square Dinner & Stroll
description: Denver's oldest block, strung with lights year-round. Pick a restaurant, then wander the historic facades arm in arm.

title: Avanti Rooftop, LoHi
description: A collective eatery of food vendors with a rooftop deck framing the downtown skyline. Sunset cocktails and skyline views in LoHi.

title: Red Rocks Amphitheatre Concert
description: There's no venue like it on earth — live music between 300-foot sandstone monoliths under the stars. Check the calendar and grab tickets.

title: Denver Art Museum Late Night
description: Wander the Hamilton building's galleries after dark with a glass of wine. First-Friday energy without the daytime crowds.

title: Meow Wolf — Convergence Station
description: Step through four wildly imaginative worlds in this surreal immersive art experience. Endlessly weird, photogenic, and conversation-fueling.

title: Colorado Symphony Date Night
description: Dress up for an evening at Boettcher Concert Hall. Pair it with a pre-show dinner downtown for full date-night polish.

title: Denver Botanic Gardens Stroll
description: 24 acres of themed gardens in the heart of the city — Japanese garden, alpine rockery, conservatory. Different every season.

title: Clyfford Still Museum
description: A serene, single-artist museum next to the DAM. Intimate, rarely crowded, and a perfect pair with coffee in the Golden Triangle.

title: Strawberry Park Hot Springs, Steamboat
description: Rustic, stone-lined natural pools tucked in the forest above Steamboat. Adults-only after dark — a true road-trip soak.

title: Mount Princeton Hot Springs
description: Soak in riverside pools with a 14er towering overhead near Buena Vista. Hotter creekside tubs for the brave.

title: Stargazing at Eleven Mile Reservoir
description: A certified International Dark Sky Park southwest of Denver. Bring a blanket and watch the Milky Way blaze over the water.

title: Board Game Café in RiNo
description: Hundreds of games, a full coffee-and-beer menu, and a guide to teach you the rules. Cozy, low-stakes, and quietly competitive.

title: Bookstore Crawl, Capitol Hill
description: Hit the Tattered Cover and a few used-book gems, swapping picks over coffee between stops. A slow, bookish afternoon.

title: Pottery Wheel Class, Art District
description: Get your hands muddy and laugh through a beginner wheel-throwing class. You'll leave with a lopsided mug and a great story.

title: RiNo Art District Mural Walk + Brunch
description: Wander blocks of bold street art, then settle into a patio brunch. The murals change constantly — there's always something new.

title: Washington Park Paddleboats + Picnic
description: Rent a paddleboat on Smith Lake, then sprawl on the grass with a picnic. Classic Denver summer, no mountains required.

title: Cocktails at Union Station
description: Sink into a leather chair in the Great Hall of Denver's beautifully restored train station. Order the Terminal Bar's signature cocktail and people-watch.

title: Golden Riverwalk + Coors Tour
description: Stroll Clear Creek through historic Golden, watch tubers float by, and cap it with a brewery tour. Mountains right at the edge of town.

title: Auraria Campus Evening Wander
description: Walk the historic Ninth Street Park and grab a bite at Tivoli — a quiet, leafy pocket of the city right by the university.

title: Rockies Game at Coors Field
description: Cheap seats, mountain views beyond the outfield, and a Rockpile sunset. Baseball is just the excuse — the vibe is the date.

title: Cherry Creek Trail Bike Ride
description: Pedal the paved creek trail from downtown to Cherry Creek State Park and back. Pack a picnic for the reservoir turnaround.

title: Indoor Climbing Date
description: Belay each other up colorful routes at a Denver climbing gym. Trust-building, low-key competitive, and a genuine workout.

title: Ski Day at Arapahoe Basin
description: Closest big terrain to Denver and the longest season in Colorado. Spring laps in t-shirts, beers at the 6th Alley after.

title: Sledding + Cocoa Afternoon
description: Grab cheap sleds and find a snowy hill in the foothills. Pure, giggly winter joy followed by warming up by a fire.

title: Mount Blue Sky Scenic Drive
description: Drive the highest paved road in North America to 14,000 ft. Mountain goats, alpine lakes, and views that go on forever.

title: Garden of the Gods + Manitou
description: Towering red rock formations against Pikes Peak, free to wander, then arcade games and salt-water taffy in quirky Manitou Springs.

title: Georgetown Loop Railroad
description: Ride a historic narrow-gauge steam train through the mountains between two old mining towns. Especially magical for the fall colors run.

title: Cave of the Winds Adventure
description: Lantern tour through limestone caverns plus a cliffside ropes course near Manitou Springs. Equal parts spooky and thrilling.

title: Sip & Paint Night
description: Follow along with an instructor, glass of wine in hand, and walk away with matching (ish) canvases. Low pressure, high laughs.

title: Retro Drive-In Movie Night
description: Snuggle up in the car at a classic Front Range drive-in. Double features, carhop snacks, and a cozy backseat blanket nest.

title: Saturday Farmers Market + Brunch
description: Sample, stroll, and fill a tote with peaches and pastries at a Front Range farmers market, then find a sunny brunch spot nearby.

title: Comedy Club Night
description: Catch a stand-up set at a Denver comedy club with a two-drink minimum and a lot of laughs. Sit near the back unless you like getting roasted.

title: Glenwood Caverns Adventure Park
description: Cliff-edge thrill rides perched 1,300 ft above the Colorado River, plus cave tours. A high-adrenaline road-trip date.

title: Glenwood Hot Springs Pool
description: The world's largest hot springs pool — block out an afternoon to float, soak, and watch the steam rise off the Colorado River.

title: Williams & Graham
description: Denver's most beloved speakeasy hides behind a working bookstore in LoHi. Slide into a leather booth for impeccable classic cocktails in a Prohibition-era hush.
entry_instructions: Walk into the tiny bookstore at 3160 Tejon St and tell the clerk you'd like a table — a hidden bookcase door swings open. Reservations strongly recommended (especially weekends); walk-ins can wait at the bar.

title: Ste. Ellie
description: A snug subterranean cocktail den beneath Stoic & Genuine at Union Station. Low light, vinyl, and a tightly curated list — ideal before or after a train-station dinner.
entry_instructions: At Union Station, find Stoic & Genuine in the Great Hall and take the staircase down beside it to the unmarked door below. No bookcase tricks — just descend and head in. Reservations help on weekends.

title: Millers & Rossi
description: Walk through a working RiNo art gallery and slip into the speakeasy hidden in back. Rotating exhibits up front, inventive craft cocktails behind the curtain.
entry_instructions: Enter the art gallery at 3542 Walnut St in RiNo and tell the attendant you're there for a drink — they'll walk you through to the bar in back. Reservations recommended; hours can be event-driven, so check ahead.

title: Retrograde
description: Step through a faux retro storefront into a sleek RiNo cocktail lounge with a playful, science-leaning menu. The disguise out front is half the fun.
entry_instructions: Look for the unassuming faux-storefront on Larimer St in RiNo and walk straight through it to reach the hidden lounge. Entrances shift with the theme — confirm the current address on their site and book ahead.

title: The Cruise Room at the Oxford Hotel
description: Denver's oldest bar — an Art Deco jewel modeled on a lounge aboard the Queen Mary, opened the day after Prohibition ended. Order a classic martini under its rosy glow.
entry_instructions: No secret door here — walk into the historic Oxford Hotel lobby at 1600 17th St (steps from Union Station) and the Cruise Room is just off it. First-come, first-served; arrive early for a booth.

title: Lookout Mountain Sunset Drive
description: Wind up to Buffalo Bill's grave for a panoramic Front Range sunset. Short, scenic, and ten minutes from Golden's breweries.

title: Elitch Gardens Day
description: Roller coasters, a water park in summer, and funnel cake in the heart of the city. Bring out your inner kid together.

title: Couples Cooking Class
description: Learn to make fresh pasta or sushi side by side, then sit down to eat what you made. Delicious, hands-on, and a little competitive.

title: Denver Zoo Lights
description: Wander the zoo after dark beneath millions of lights and glowing animal sculptures. Cocoa in hand, crisp winter air, total wonder.

title: Record Shopping + Specialty Coffee
description: Flip through crates at a Denver record store, then debate your finds over pour-overs at a nearby roaster. Slow, analog, and easy.

title: Boozy Mini Golf at Puttshack
description: Tech-tracked mini golf with cocktails and globally-inspired snacks. Trash talk encouraged; keeping score optional.

title: Bear Creek Lake Park Paddle
description: Rent a kayak or paddleboard on a quiet reservoir minutes from the city, with the foothills as a backdrop. Pack lunch for the shore.

title: Boulder Dushanbe Teahouse
description: Sip rare teas inside a hand-carved Tajik teahouse — a gift from Boulder's sister city. Order the afternoon tea service and slow way down.

title: Pearl Street Mall Evening
description: Street performers, patio dining, and indie shops along Boulder's pedestrian mall. Easy, lively, and endlessly strollable.

title: Guided Snowshoe Trek
description: Strap on snowshoes and crunch through a silent, snow-blanketed forest in the high country. A naturalist-led trek makes it effortless.

title: Planetarium Show + Museum
description: Lean back for a dome show at the Denver Museum of Nature & Science, then explore the gems, mummies, and dino halls. Rainy-day perfect.
